question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCall with a crafted receiver address that causes the transaction body type selection logic (isContractCreate check) to misclassify the call as a contract create, resulting in an incorrect TransactionBody type being executed and an incorrect record exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a ContractDebugParameters with a non-null but zero-length ethereumData byte array to bypass the Ethereum transaction body path and fall through to the contract create or call path, causing the mirror node to receive a record with an incorrect transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger execute() with estimatedGas=0 so that the built TransactionBody carries zero gas, causing the EVM execution to fail silently and produce a missing or incomplete record exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user cause singleTransactionRecords to be an empty list by crafting a transaction that produces no records, causing getFirst() to throw an uncaught exception that prevents any record from being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildSuccessResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call that produces child transactions with failures while the parent succeeds, so that populateChildTransactionErrors() collects errors that are only logged as warnings and never exported as part of the mirror node record, causing missing child failure records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildSuccessResult()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where getTransactionResult() throws contractCreateResultOrThrow() or contractCallResultOrThrow() due to a missing result field, causing an exception that prevents the record from being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildSuccessResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where the parent record has SUCCESS status but contractID in the result has neither contractNum nor evmAddress set, causing the warning log to emit null/empty contract identifiers and the mirror node to receive an ambiguous or incorrect record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a failed transaction where result is null (pre-check failure) so that the MirrorEvmTransactionException is thrown with empty detail and data, causing the mirror node to export a record with no meaningful error information?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user set up an opcode trace context (ContractCallContext.get().getOpcodeContext() != null) for a failed transaction so that handleFailedResult() returns an EvmTransactionResult instead of throwing MirrorEvmTransactionException, causing the failed transaction to be silently treated as a non-exceptional result and potentially exported as a success record to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a failed contract create where parentTransactionRecord.contractCreateResult() returns null but isContractCreate is true, causing the null result path to throw MirrorEvmTransactionException with empty strings, resulting in a mirror node record that omits the actual revert reason?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction whose error message does not start with the HEX_PREFIX so that getErrorMessage() returns Optional.empty(), causing maybeDecodeSolidityErrorStringToReadableMessage to receive only '0x' and the mirror node record to export an empty or misleading error detail?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user supply ethereumData with a crafted nonce value that differs from the stored account nonce, causing patchSenderNonce() to overwrite the account's ethereumNonce in the write cache, resulting in the mirror node receiving a record that reflects an incorrect nonce state for the sender account?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user craft ethereumData where populateEthTxData() returns a nonce of 0 while the stored account nonce is non-zero, causing patchSenderNonce() to reset the nonce to 0 in the write cache and the mirror node to export a record with an incorrect sender nonce?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sender address that is zero with a non-zero value, bypassing the early return in patchSenderNonce() (params.getSender().isZero() && params.getValue() == 0L), causing unintended nonce patching and an incorrect account state record exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractDebugParameters where ContractCallContext.isInitialized() returns false, causing patchSenderNonce() to return early without patching, so that the transaction executes with a mismatched nonce and the mirror node exports a record that does not reflect the actual nonce used?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user supply malformed ethereumData that causes populateEthTxData() to throw an exception inside patchSenderNonce(), resulting in the transaction aborting before any record is produced and the mirror node missing the record entirely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sender address that is zero with value=0 so that getSenderAccountID() substitutes the treasury account as the sender, causing the mirror node to export a record that incorrectly attributes the transaction to the treasury account instead of the actual caller?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sender address that resolves to a smart contract account, causing getSenderAccountID() to substitute the treasury account as the payer in the TransactionID, resulting in the mirror node exporting a record with an incorrect payer account?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a hollow account address (account with no key or IMMUTABILITY_SENTINEL_KEY) so that getSenderAccountID() completes the account in the write cache with DEFAULT_KEY, causing the mirror node to export a record reflecting a key that was never set on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sender address for an account that does not exist in accountReadableKVState, causing throwPayerAccountNotFoundException() to be called with PAYER_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND, resulting in the mirror node receiving no record for the attempted transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a non-long-zero EVM address that is not present in aliasesReadableKVState, causing throwPayerAccountNotFoundException() to be invoked and the transaction to abort without producing any mirror node record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a long-zero address that encodes a non-existent account number, causing accountIdFromEvmAddress() to produce an AccountID that does not correspond to any real account, and the mirror node to export a record with a fabricated sender account ID?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a null sender address so that ConversionUtils.isLongZero(null) behaves unexpectedly, causing an incorrect branch to be taken in getSenderAccountIDAsNum() and the mirror node to receive a record with a wrong sender AccountID?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger defaultTransactionBodyBuilder() at a time when Instant.now() produces a transactionValidStart that conflicts with an existing transaction ID, causing the executor to reject the transaction and produce no mirror node record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()] Can an unprivileged external user craft parameters such that getSenderAccountID() returns the treasury account as the payer, causing the TransactionID to embed the treasury account, and the mirror node to export a record that incorrectly identifies the treasury as the transaction initiator?
